title: Number of one-year-olds who are not vaccinated against measles
caption:
Number of one-year-olds who are not vaccinated against measles

This choropleth world map displays the estimated number of one-year-olds who were not vaccinated against measles in 2023. The legend uses a color scale ranging from light beige for zero unvaccinated children to dark red for one million, with intermediate thresholds at 10,000, 30,000, 100,000, and 300,000. The darkest red shades, indicating the highest counts of unvaccinated children, are concentrated in specific regions of Africa and Asia. Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, India, and Pakistan fall into the highest visible bands, with values extending into the 300,000 to 1 million range. Surrounding countries in West and East Africa, as well as parts of Southeast Asia like Indonesia and the Philippines, appear in mid-range orange and red shades that sit between the 30,000 and 300,000 thresholds. In the Americas, Brazil and Mexico show darker orange shades compared to their neighbors, placing them in the tens of thousands range. North America, most of Europe, and Australia sit predominantly in the lightest shades of the legend, indicating lower absolute numbers of unvaccinated one-year-olds. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data. The visual pattern shows the highest absolute numbers of unvaccinated children clustered in populous nations across Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia. Measles is a highly contagious viral disease, most common in young children, and its effects include blindness, inflammation of the brain, severe diarrhea, and severe respiratory infections such as pneumonia.
